hero.heading: Build It. Ship It.

faq.heading: Common questions
faq.subheading: Everything you need to know about CapiBot.

items.a: CapiBot is a private AI agent orchestration platform. Every user gets their own dedicated instance with 280+ specialized AI agents that can collaborate, execute tasks, and form autonomous AI companies — all running on infrastructure that's exclusively yours.

All plans include every feature, every AI model, and unlimited agents and companies. The only difference between plans is the number of monthly credits. Standard models cost 1 credit per action, while premium models like Sonnet (5 credits) and Opus (10 credits) cost more. You choose how to spend your credits.

items.q: What AI models can I use?
items.a: All of them — on every plan. CapiBot supports Claude (Haiku, Sonnet, Opus), GPT-4o, Google Gemini, Kimi, Qwen, GLM, MiniMax, and local models via Ollama. You can configure priority order and automatic failover between providers.

AI Companies are CapiBot's flagship feature. A CEO agent drafts a business plan, hires specialist agents from a roster of 280+, creates tasks, and executes — with configurable human oversight at every step.

In managed mode, all CEO-created tasks require human approval before execution. You can approve plans, hiring decisions, and budgets through the Mission Control dashboard. Or switch to fully autonomous mode when you're confident.
Telegram, WhatsApp, Slack, and a built-in web chat in Mission Control. Each channel supports rich messages, media, and voice transcription where applicable.
Completely. Every user gets their own dedicated instance with its own database. Your data is never shared with other users. There's no telemetry, no data collection, and no shared infrastructure.

Credits are consumed when your agents take actions that require an AI model — sending messages, processing tasks, making decisions. System operations like heartbeat checks and scheduled monitoring are free and don't use credits.

items.q: How do I connect external apps?
items.a: Through Composio, which provides access to thousands of apps via a unified API including Gmail, Google Docs, Notion, and more. You can also write custom skills as Markdown files — no code changes needed.
Pick a plan, complete checkout, choose your username, and your private instance is live within 60 seconds at username.capibot.io. No setup, no configuration, no ops work required.

openClaw.heading: CapiBot vs OpenClaw
openClaw.description: CapiBot and OpenClaw are both open-source, self-hosted AI assistant platforms. They take very different approaches to who they are for. OpenClaw is a CLI-driven personal assistant built for technical users who want to tinker with Node, daemons, and config files. CapiBot is a web app your grandma could use — but powerful enough to run entire AI companies with teams of agents.

shortVersion.title: Zero setup
shortVersion.desc: CapiBot is a web app. Sign in, pick a template, go. OpenClaw needs Node 24, a global npm install, a Gateway daemon, and a pairing code for every messaging channel you want to connect.

shortVersion.title: A team, not a single assistant
shortVersion.desc: CapiBot ships with 281 pre-built agents across 18 categories — organized as CEOs, specialists, and interns. OpenClaw is a single personal assistant by design.

shortVersion.title: Run AI Companies
shortVersion.desc: CapiBot has a full company lifecycle: briefing, planning, hiring, budgets, and human approval at every gate. OpenClaw has no concept of a multi-agent company.

openClaw.whySubtitle: Six reasons non-technical founders pick CapiBot over OpenClaw.

advantages.title: No terminal required
advantages.description: CapiBot runs in your browser. OpenClaw is installed with `npm install -g openclaw`, configured through CLI commands, and diagnosed with `openclaw doctor` when something breaks. If that sentence intimidated you, CapiBot is for you.

advantages.title: A pre-built team of 281 agents
advantages.description: Hire a market researcher, a technical writer, an SDR, or a full team of them. Each agent is pre-loaded with the skills and tools it needs. OpenClaw gives you one personal assistant and expects you to configure it yourself.

advantages.title: AI Companies with one click
advantages.description: 16 company templates cover SaaS founder, consulting firm, content studio, and more. Pick one and CapiBot deploys a CEO, hires the team, and starts planning. OpenClaw has no equivalent.

advantages.title: Mission Control dashboard
advantages.description: A 28-panel web dashboard for every agent, task, company, credit, and error trend in one place. OpenClaw's control plane is a Gateway WebSocket daemon with a basic Web UI.

advantages.title: Managed by default
advantages.description: Every plan, hire, and budget decision your AI team makes comes to you for approval before it executes. Review it, edit it, or send it back. You are the boss.

advantages.title: Multi-provider failover
advantages.description: Automatic failover across 6 LLM providers with configurable priority. If Claude is down, your agents switch to Kimi. If Kimi is down, they fall back to local Ollama.

openClaw.comparisonSubtitle: An honest comparison. Both projects are open source, both are self-hosted, both work well for what they are built for — but they are built for different people.
forYouItems: You do not want to install Node, run terminal commands, or restart a daemon
You want an AI team — not a single personal assistant
You want to deploy a full AI company with one click
You want human oversight on plans, hires, and budgets without micromanaging every action
You need a real web dashboard, not a CLI
You want multi-provider LLM support with automatic failover
You are a founder first, not a systems admin
openClaw.ctaDesc: Join the waitlist for early access and founding member pricing. CapiBot is launching soon.
